<!-- Title: pandoc_writing_tools Feature Demo -->

<ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="">sec:overview</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><h1 id="sec:overview">1 Overview</h1>

<p>This document demonstrates the functionality of the Lua filters in pandoc_writing_tools.</p>

<ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="">sec:citations-and-refs</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><h2 id="sec:citations-and-refs">1.1 Citations and section references</h2>

<p>This is what a citation looks like: <cite>(Doe 2020)</cite>. The list of all cited references appears in the bibliography section at the end of the document.</p>

<p>The pandoc_writing_tools lua filters enable referring to a section by number, for example, this references section <ac:link ac:anchor="sec:overview"><ac:link-body>1</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</p>

<ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="">table-of-contents</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><h2 id="table-of-contents">1.2 Table of contents</h2>

<p>The table of contents is generated from the section headers. The <code>toc-list-top-level</code> and <code>toc-list-entry-levels</code> attributes control which headers are included and how they are nested. For example, the mini-TOC below includes level 2 and level 3 headers under the “Overview” section:</p>

<div style="margin-left: 40px;"><ac:link ac:anchor="sec:citations-and-refs"><ac:link-body>1.1 Citations and section references</ac:link-body></ac:link><br/>

<ac:link ac:anchor="table-of-contents"><ac:link-body>1.2 Table of contents</ac:link-body></ac:link><br/>

<ac:link ac:anchor="sec:figures"><ac:link-body>1.3 Figures and references</ac:link-body></ac:link><br/>

<ac:link ac:anchor="sec:examples"><ac:link-body>1.4 Examples and definitions</ac:link-body></ac:link><br/>

<ac:link ac:anchor="sec:index-entries"><ac:link-body>1.5 Index entries</ac:link-body></ac:link><br/>

<ac:link ac:anchor="sec:sidenotes"><ac:link-body>1.6 Sidenotes</ac:link-body></ac:link><br/>

<ac:link ac:anchor="sec:todos"><ac:link-body>1.7 TODOs</ac:link-body></ac:link><br/>

<ac:link ac:anchor="sec:github-links"><ac:link-body>1.8 Linking to github issues and edit links</ac:link-body></ac:link><br/>
</div>

<ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="">sec:figures</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><h2 id="sec:figures">1.3 Figures and references</h2>

<ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="">fig:demo</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="align" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="align">center</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel">
<ac:parameter ac:name="bgColor">#FFFFFF</ac:parameter>
<ac:parameter ac:name="title">
Figure 1: A demo figure
</ac:parameter>
<ac:rich-text-body>
<figure>
<ac:image ac:width="440"><ri:attachment ri:filename="img/demo-figure.png"/></ac:image>
</figure>
</ac:rich-text-body>
</ac:structured-macro>
</ac:rich-text-body>
</ac:structured-macro>

<p>See Figure <ac:link ac:anchor="fig:demo"><ac:link-body>1</ac:link-body></ac:link> for the numbered figure reference.</p>

<ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="">sec:examples</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><h2 id="sec:examples">1.4 Examples and definitions</h2>

<p>We have custom environments for examples and definitions, which are numbered and can be referenced from the text:</p>

<ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="">ex:demo</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example 1: Demo example</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>This block is tagged as an example.</p>
</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="">def:demo</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Definition 1: Demo definition</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>This block is tagged as a definition.</p>
</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<p>Refer back to Example <ac:link ac:anchor="ex:demo"><ac:link-body>1</ac:link-body></ac:link> and Definition <ac:link ac:anchor="def:demo"><ac:link-body>1</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</p>

<ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="">sec:index-entries</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><h2 id="sec:index-entries">1.5 Index entries</h2>

<p>The index filter allows you to mark index entries in the text, which are then collected and emitted in an index div, see at the end of this document. The simplest usage is to just add <code>.index</code> spans around the relevant text and add an index div somewhere in the document. The filter will collect all <code>.index</code> spans and emit them as entries in the index div. You can also specify an <code>entry</code> attribute on the span to customize the index entry text, and use <code>!</code> to create nested entries.</p>

<p>For example, in this sentence the word <ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="">__index_entry_1</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><span id="__index_entry_1" class="index">color</span> is indexed, and a we index the word “concept” as “idea”. We can also create nested entries like <ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="">__index_entry_3</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><span id="__index_entry_3" class="index">color!blue</span>, and multiple entries like <ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="">__index_entry_4</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><span id="__index_entry_4" class="index">alpha;beta</span>.</p>

<ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="">sec:sidenotes</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><h2 id="sec:sidenotes">1.6 Sidenotes</h2>

<p>This sentence has a footnote.<sup>[1]</sup>
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="float" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="side">right</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="width">150px</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body>
  <ac:structured-macro ac:name="bgcolor" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="color">#FFFCF2</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body>
<sup>[1]</sup><p>Footnote content that becomes a sidenote in HTML.</p>

  </ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></p>

<ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="">sec:todos</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><h2 id="sec:todos">1.7 TODOs</h2>

<p>It is often useful to be able to mark TODO items in the text. It helps you to avoid writer’s block on something you can’t quite put into words just yet. The <code>markup_todo.lua</code> filter allows you to do this with a <code>.todo</code> class. You can use this class on inline spans for inline TODOs, or on divs for block TODOs. For example, please <span style="background-color: yellow; color: red; font-weight: bold;">TODO: fix this</span> inline.</p>

<div style="background-color: yellow; color: red; font-weight: bold;">TODO: Block TODO content.</div>

<ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="">sec:github-links</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><h2 id="sec:github-links">1.8 Linking to github issues and edit links</h2>

<p>Github issue link when enabled: Github issue <span class="issue">1</span>.</p>

<p>Edit links appear as little pencil icons at the end of headers when <code>add_edit_to_headers.lua</code> is enabled.</p>

<ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="">index-header</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><h1 id="index-header" class="unnumbered">Index</h1>

</ac:layout-cell>
</ac:layout-section>
<ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal">
<ac:layout-cell>
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel">
<ac:parameter ac:name="bgColor">#EFEFEF</ac:parameter>
<ac:rich-text-body>
<span class="index-entry-text">alpha</span> <span class="index-entry-locator"> <ac:link ac:anchor="__index_entry_4"><ac:link-body>1.5</ac:link-body></ac:link></span><br/>

<br/>

<span class="index-entry-text">beta</span> <span class="index-entry-locator"> <ac:link ac:anchor="__index_entry_4"><ac:link-body>1.5</ac:link-body></ac:link></span><br/>

<br/>

<span class="index-entry-text">color</span> <span class="index-entry-locator"> <ac:link ac:anchor="__index_entry_1"><ac:link-body>1.5</ac:link-body></ac:link></span></ac:rich-text-body>
</ac:structured-macro>
</ac:layout-cell>
<ac:layout-cell>
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel">
<ac:parameter ac:name="bgColor">#EFEFEF</ac:parameter>
<ac:rich-text-body>


&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;<span class="index-entry-text">blue</span> <span class="index-entry-locator"> <ac:link ac:anchor="__index_entry_3"><ac:link-body>1.5</ac:link-body></ac:link></span><br/>

<br/>

<span class="index-entry-text">idea</span> <span class="index-entry-locator"> <ac:link ac:anchor="__index_entry_2"><ac:link-body>1.5</ac:link-body></ac:link></span><br/>
</ac:rich-text-body>
</ac:structured-macro>
</ac:layout-cell>
</ac:layout-section>
<ac:layout-section ac:type="single">
<ac:layout-cell>

<ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="">refs</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><div id="refs" class="references csl-bib-body hanging-indent">
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:parameter ac:name="">ref-doe2020</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel">
  <ac:parameter ac:name="bgColor">#EFEFEF</ac:parameter>
  <ac:rich-text-body>
<p>Doe, Jane. 2020. <span>“Demo Reference.”</span> <em>Demo Journal</em>.</p>
  </ac:rich-text-body>
</ac:structured-macro>

</div> </ac:layout-cell>
</ac:layout-section>
